meaning_en: Completely and without doubt; used for emphasis
example: I absolutely love the food here.
mnemonic: ABSOLUTELY — ABSOLUTE + LY; absolute certainty = absolutely.

meaning_en: The right or ability to use or enter something
example: Students have access to the library 24 hours a day.
mnemonic: ACCESS — AC + CESS; success begins with access.

meaning_en: A place where someone can stay or live
example: The university provides accommodation for first-year students.
mnemonic: ACCOMMODATION — ACCOMMODATE + ION; a place that accommodates you.

meaning_en: A record of money; or a description of events
example: She opened a bank account to save her salary.
mnemonic: ACCOUNT — ACCOUNT-able; you account for every rupee.

meaning_en: Something done successfully through effort
example: Winning the gold medal was her greatest achievement.
mnemonic: ACHIEVEMENT — ACHIEVE + MENT; the result of achieving something.

meaning_en: To respect and approve of someone or something
example: I admire her dedication to helping others.
mnemonic: ADMIRE — AD + MIRE; a mirror (MIRE) reflects greatness — you admire it.

meaning_en: To accept that something is true; to allow entry
example: He admitted that he had made a mistake.
mnemonic: ADMIT — AD + MIT; MIT only admits the best students.

meaning_en: To have enough money or time for something
example: We can't afford a holiday this year.
mnemonic: AFFORD — A FORD car; you can afford a Ford, but not a Ferrari.

meaning_en: A goal or purpose; to direct something towards a target
example: My aim is to become a software engineer.
mnemonic: AIM — an ARROW AIMed at a target hits the bullseye.

meaning_en: A warning sound; a feeling of fear about danger
example: The fire alarm rang and everyone left the building.
mnemonic: ALARM — A + LARM; an ARM raising ALARM wakes you up!

meaning_en: A different option or choice available
example: If you don't like tea, there is an alternative — juice.
mnemonic: ALTERNATIVE — ALTER + NATIVE; alter the native path = take an alternative.

meaning_en: Having a strong desire to succeed
example: She is ambitious and plans to start her own business.
mnemonic: AMBITIOUS — AMBITION + S; someone full of ambitions is ambitious.

meaning_en: To make a public or official statement
example: The school announced the results on Friday.
mnemonic: ANNOUNCE — A + NOUN + CE; put a NOUN (name) out for everyone to hear.

meaning_en: To say sorry for something you have done wrong
example: She apologised for being late to the meeting.
mnemonic: APOLOGISE — APOLOGY + ISE; making an apology = apologising.

meaning_en: To recognise the value or importance of something
example: I really appreciate your help with this project.
mnemonic: APPRECIATE — A PRICE + ATE; you appreciate the true PRICE of things.

meaning_en: To take someone into legal custody by police
example: The police arrested the thief near the market.
mnemonic: ARREST — A + REST; when arrested, you are put to rest in a cell.

meaning_en: A task given to someone as part of their work or studies
example: The teacher gave us an assignment on climate change.
mnemonic: ASSIGNMENT — ASSIGN + MENT; what is assigned to you.

meaning_en: The air around the earth; the mood of a place
example: The restaurant had a wonderful atmosphere.
mnemonic: ATMOSPHERE — ATMOS + SPHERE; the sphere of atoms around Earth.

meaning_en: A way of thinking or feeling about something
example: A positive attitude helps you overcome difficulties.
mnemonic: ATTITUDE — ALTITUDE; your attitude determines your altitude in life.

meaning_en: The power to give orders or make decisions
example: The teacher has the authority to set class rules.
mnemonic: AUTHORITY — AUTHOR + ITY; an AUTHOR has the right to control the story.

meaning_en: The most important and fundamental; simple
example: The school teaches basic reading and writing skills.
mnemonic: BASIC — BASE + IC; the BASE of everything = basic.

meaning_en: A fight between groups; a struggle to achieve something
example: The doctors fought a long battle against the disease.
mnemonic: BATTLE — a RATTLE of swords in BATTLE.

meaning_en: The way a person or animal acts
example: Good behaviour is expected from all students.
mnemonic: BEHAVIOUR — BEHAVE + YOUR; your way of behaving.

meaning_en: Willing to face danger without showing fear
example: The brave firefighter rescued the child from the flames.
mnemonic: BRAVE — a WAVE can be conquered by a BRAVE surfer.

meaning_en: An organised effort to achieve a goal, especially political
example: The government launched a campaign to promote cleanliness.
mnemonic: CAMPAIGN — CAMP + AIGN; setting up CAMP to fight for a cause.

meaning_en: A job or profession that someone does over a long period
example: She chose a career in medicine.
mnemonic: CAREER — a CAR that needs CARE to keep moving forward.

meaning_en: A group of things with similar features
example: This book falls into the category of science fiction.
mnemonic: CATEGORY — CATE + GORY; file stories into categories like a library.

meaning_en: A difficult task that tests your ability
example: Learning a new language is a real challenge.
mnemonic: CHALLENGE — CHANCE + BELL; ring the bell — chance to face a challenge!

meaning_en: The winner of a competition; someone who defends a cause
example: He became the national chess champion at 16.
mnemonic: CHAMPION — CHAMP + ION; an ion of energy fuels a champ.

meaning_en: To say something is true; to demand something as your right
example: She claimed that she had finished the work on time.
mnemonic: CLAIM — EXCLAIM; to claim something loudly is to exclaim it.

meaning_en: Feeling sure about yourself or something
example: She felt confident before her presentation.
mnemonic: CONFIDENT — CON + FIDENT; having full FAITH (fident) in yourself.

meaning_en: To make someone unable to understand clearly
example: The two roads looked so similar they confused him.
mnemonic: CONFUSE — CON + FUSE; mix the FUSES up and everything gets confused.

meaning_en: Land outside towns and cities with fields and nature
example: She loves the quiet and beauty of the countryside.
mnemonic: COUNTRYSIDE — COUNTRY + SIDE; the side of the country away from cities.

meaning_en: Causing pain or suffering deliberately
example: It is cruel to leave animals without food or water.
mnemonic: CRUEL — a DUEL can be cruel when fought without mercy.

meaning_en: The customs, beliefs, and arts of a group of people
example: India has a rich and diverse culture.
mnemonic: CULTURE — CULTIVATE; you cultivate culture over generations.

meaning_en: Physical harm that makes something less useful or valuable
example: The flood caused serious damage to the village.
mnemonic: DAMAGE — DAM + AGE; a broken dam causes damage over time.

meaning_en: A period of ten years
example: Technology has changed a lot over the past decade.
mnemonic: DECADE — DECA means ten; a decade = 10 years.

meaning_en: The place you are travelling to
example: Paris is one of the most popular tourist destinations.
mnemonic: DESTINATION — DESTINY + NATION; your destiny within a nation.

meaning_en: Having a strong will to achieve something
example: She was determined to pass the exam no matter what.
mnemonic: DETERMINED — DETERMINE + D; once determined, the decision (D) is final.

meaning_en: Feeling sad because something was not as good as expected
example: He was disappointed when he didn't get the scholarship.
mnemonic: DISAPPOINTED — DIS + APPOINT; when hopes fail to meet the appointment.

meaning_en: To talk about something in detail with others
example: Let's discuss the plan before we start.
mnemonic: DISCUSS — DISC + USS; toss a DISC of ideas between us = discuss.

meaning_en: A film or programme presenting real events or facts
example: We watched a documentary about wild animals in Africa.
mnemonic: DOCUMENTARY — DOCUMENT + ARY; documenting real events = documentary.

meaning_en: A sudden violent shaking of the ground
example: The earthquake damaged hundreds of buildings.
mnemonic: EARTHQUAKE — EARTH + QUAKE; the EARTH QUAKEs and shakes.

meaning_en: The system of trade and money in a country
example: India has one of the fastest-growing economies in the world.
mnemonic: ECONOMY — ECO + NOMY; managing the eco(system) of money.

meaning_en: The process of teaching and learning in schools or colleges
example: Education is the key to a better future.
mnemonic: EDUCATION — EDUCATE + ION; being educated throughout.

meaning_en: A result or change caused by something
example: The medicine had an immediate effect on her headache.
mnemonic: EFFECT — the E after CAUSE = EFFECT; causes always create effects.

meaning_en: The energy used to do something
example: She put a lot of effort into preparing for the exam.
mnemonic: EFFORT — an EFFORT is when you FORT-ify your willpower.

meaning_en: The process of choosing leaders by voting
example: Millions of people voted in the general election.
mnemonic: ELECTION — ELECT + ION; people ELECT someone via the election process.

meaning_en: A sudden dangerous situation requiring immediate action
example: Call the ambulance — this is an emergency!
mnemonic: EMERGENCY — EMERGE + ENCY; when danger EMERGES, it's an emergency.

meaning_en: To give someone confidence or support to do something
example: Her teacher encouraged her to enter the competition.
mnemonic: ENCOURAGE — EN + COURAGE; fill someone with COURAGE.

meaning_en: The natural world; the conditions around you
example: We must protect the environment for future generations.
mnemonic: ENVIRONMENT — ENVIRON + MENT; everything that environs (surrounds) us.

meaning_en: To get free from a dangerous or unpleasant situation
example: The prisoner tried to escape through the back window.
mnemonic: ESCAPE — ES + CAPE; put on a CAPE and fly away to escape!

meaning_en: Something that happens, especially something planned
example: The music festival is the biggest event of the year.
mnemonic: EVENT — EVENTually things happen; an event is a happening.

meaning_en: To look at carefully or test someone
example: The doctor examined the patient thoroughly.
mnemonic: EXAMINE — EXAM + INE; examining leads to an exam result.

meaning_en: To travel through an unfamiliar area to learn about it
example: She loves to explore new cities when she travels.
mnemonic: EXPLORE — EX + PLORE; going beyond the shore to explore.

meaning_en: Extremely interesting and attractive
example: The documentary about deep-sea creatures was fascinating.
mnemonic: FASCINATING — FASCINATE + ING; if something fascinates you, it's fascinating.

meaning_en: An important or interesting part of something
example: The best feature of this phone is its camera.
mnemonic: FEATURE — FEAT + URE; a feature is a notable FEAT of a product.

meaning_en: Relating to money and finances
example: She is facing financial difficulties after losing her job.
mnemonic: FINANCIAL — FINANCE + AL; all about finance and money.

meaning_en: A large amount of water covering an area; an overwhelming amount
example: Heavy rains caused a flood in the lower parts of the city.
mnemonic: FLOOD — BLOOD; a flood of water is like blood flowing through earth.

meaning_en: Power or strength; to make someone do something
example: The strong wind forced us to stay indoors.
mnemonic: FORCE — FORCE of nature; you can't resist a natural force.

meaning_en: A close relationship between people who care for each other
example: True friendship is built on trust and respect.
mnemonic: FRIENDSHIP — FRIEND + SHIP; a SHIP that carries your FRIEND safely.

meaning_en: A substance that produces energy when burned
example: The car ran out of fuel on the highway.
mnemonic: FUEL — FULL; a FULL tank of fuel keeps you going.

meaning_en: All the people born and living at the same time
example: The younger generation is more comfortable with technology.
mnemonic: GENERATION — GENERATE + ION; what a generation generates for the next.

meaning_en: Willing to give more than expected
example: She is generous and always helps those in need.
mnemonic: GENEROUS — GENUS; a noble genus (type) of person is generous.

meaning_en: Relating to the whole world
example: Climate change is a global problem.
mnemonic: GLOBAL — GLOBE + AL; relating to the whole globe.

meaning_en: The group of people that officially controls a country
example: The government announced free healthcare for all citizens.
mnemonic: GOVERNMENT — GOVERN + MENT; the mental framework to govern a nation.

meaning_en: The process of increasing in size or importance
example: The country showed strong economic growth last year.
mnemonic: GROWTH — GROW + TH; the result of growing.

meaning_en: Responsible for a crime or wrongdoing; feeling shame
example: The court found him guilty of theft.
mnemonic: GUILTY — GUILT + Y; why (Y) is there GUILT? Because you're guilty.

meaning_en: Famous or important in history
example: The signing of the agreement was a historic moment.
mnemonic: HISTORIC — HISTORY + C; a key moment in HISTORY = historic.

meaning_en: Truthful and not likely to cheat or lie
example: Be honest with me — what do you really think?
mnemonic: HONEST — HONOR + EST; the most honourable is the most honest.

meaning_en: To deliberately pay no attention to something
example: She ignored his calls for three days.
mnemonic: IGNORE — I + GNORE; when you IGNORE someone, they're a goner to you.

meaning_en: Not allowed by law
example: It is illegal to drive without a licence.
mnemonic: ILLEGAL — ILL + LEGAL; something legally ILL = illegal.

meaning_en: To make something better
example: She practises daily to improve her English.
mnemonic: IMPROVE — IM + PROVE; to prove yourself better = improve.

meaning_en: The effect someone or something has on another
example: Music has a strong influence on people's moods.
mnemonic: INFLUENCE — IN + FLUENCE; flow IN to someone's thinking = influence.

meaning_en: One of the items used to make a food dish or mixture
example: The main ingredient in this curry is tomato.
mnemonic: INGREDIENT — IN + GREED + ENT; what goes IN the greedy dish.

meaning_en: Not guilty; not having done anything wrong
example: The lawyer proved that her client was innocent.
mnemonic: INNOCENT — INNOCENCE + T; full of innocence = innocent.

meaning_en: An important topic or problem to deal with
example: Climate change is one of the biggest issues today.
mnemonic: ISSUE — IS + SUE; if there's an issue, someone will sue!

meaning_en: The act of travelling from one place to another
example: The journey from Delhi to Mumbai takes about two hours by plane.
mnemonic: JOURNEY — a JOURNAL is kept during a long JOURNEY.

meaning_en: Very enthusiastic or eager to do something
example: She is keen to learn new things every day.
mnemonic: KEEN — like a sharp edge; keenly interested = sharp focus.

meaning_en: Understanding and information gained through experience
example: A doctor needs deep knowledge of medicine.
mnemonic: KNOWLEDGE — KNOW + LEDGE; stand on the ledge of knowing.

meaning_en: A room with scientific equipment for experiments
example: Students carried out experiments in the chemistry laboratory.
mnemonic: LABORATORY — LABOR + ATORY; a place where you labor (work) on science.

meaning_en: The state of not having enough of something
example: The project failed due to a lack of funding.
mnemonic: LACK — BLACK; everything goes BLACK when you lack light.

meaning_en: A rule made by the government that people must follow
example: Everyone must obey the law of the country.
mnemonic: LAW — a FLAW in society is fixed by the LAW.

meaning_en: A covering or thickness over something else
example: Add a thin layer of cream on top of the cake.
mnemonic: LAYER — PLAYER; each PLAYER adds a LAYER to the game.

meaning_en: Allowed by law; relating to the law
example: You must have a legal permit to sell food on the street.
mnemonic: LEGAL — REGAL; a regal and lawful order is legal.

meaning_en: Free time when you are not working
example: He spends his leisure time reading and gardening.
mnemonic: LEISURE — PLEASE + URE; leisure pleases you.

meaning_en: The greatest amount allowed; a point beyond which you cannot go
example: There is a speed limit of 60 km/h on this road.
mnemonic: LIMIT — LIMb IT; cut the limb at the limit.

meaning_en: A particular place or position
example: The hotel is in a beautiful location near the beach.
mnemonic: LOCATION — LOCATE + ION; where you LOCATE something is its location.

meaning_en: Sad because you are without company
example: She felt lonely after moving to a new city.
mnemonic: LONELY — ONLY + one; only one = lonely.

meaning_en: Something expensive and very comfortable but not necessary
example: Staying in a five-star hotel is a real luxury.
mnemonic: LUXURY — LUSH + URY; lush and comfortable = luxury.

meaning_en: The control or organisation of something
example: Good time management helps you achieve more each day.
mnemonic: MANAGEMENT — MANAGE + MENT; mental planning to manage things.

meaning_en: The legal relationship between two people as husband and wife
example: They had a small marriage ceremony with close family.
mnemonic: MARRIAGE — MARRY + AGE; across all ages, people marry = marriage.

meaning_en: To find the size or amount of something; a plan of action
example: Measure the room before buying new furniture.
mnemonic: MEASURE — TREASURE; you MEASURE how much TREASURE you have.

meaning_en: Relating to the mind and thinking
example: Good mental health is as important as physical health.
mnemonic: MENTAL — MENTALITY; your mentality is your mental state.

meaning_en: Something strange that cannot be explained
example: The disappearance of the explorer remains a mystery.
mnemonic: MYSTERY — MIST + ERY; a MIST covers a mystery; it's hidden.

meaning_en: A country and its people
example: India is a democratic nation with a rich heritage.
mnemonic: NATION — NATIVE + ON; a nation is made up of its native people.

meaning_en: Not good; expressing opposition; below zero
example: Try not to have a negative attitude towards problems.
mnemonic: NEGATIVE — NEGATE + IVE; to negate means to say no — negative.

meaning_en: The area around where someone lives
example: It is a peaceful neighbourhood with many trees.
mnemonic: NEIGHBOURHOOD — NEIGHBOUR + HOOD; the hood (area) of your neighbours.

meaning_en: A special event or a particular time when something happens
example: Diwali is a special occasion celebrated across India.
mnemonic: OCCASION — OCEAN; a special occasion is as grand as an ocean.

meaning_en: What you personally think about something
example: In my opinion, this is the best book of the year.
mnemonic: OPINION — ONION; like an ONION, an OPINION has many layers.

meaning_en: A situation in which it is possible to do something
example: Studying abroad is a great opportunity.
mnemonic: OPPORTUNITY — OPPORT + UNITY; unity of the right moment = opportunity.

meaning_en: A very strong feeling or enthusiasm for something
example: She has a great passion for painting.
mnemonic: PASSION — FASHION; a passion for FASHION drives designers.

meaning_en: Able to wait calmly without getting upset
example: Be patient — the results will come with time.
mnemonic: PATIENT — WAIT + PATIENT; a patient patient waits without fuss.

meaning_en: How well someone does a task; a public show or act
example: Her performance in the play received a standing ovation.
mnemonic: PERFORMANCE — PERFORM + ANCE; the act of performing.

meaning_en: To officially allow something; an official document allowing something
example: You need a permit to park here.
mnemonic: PERMIT — PER + MIT; a permit lets you pass through the gate.

meaning_en: To cause someone to do or believe something through reasoning
example: She persuaded her parents to let her study abroad.
mnemonic: PERSUADE — PER + SUADE; swaying (suade) a person = persuade.

meaning_en: The art of taking pictures with a camera
example: She took up photography as a hobby last year.
mnemonic: PHOTOGRAPHY — PHOTO + GRAPHY; writing with light (photo) = photography.

meaning_en: Writing that expresses ideas or emotions in verse form
example: He reads poetry every morning to start his day.
mnemonic: POETRY — POET + RY; what a POET creates = poetry.

meaning_en: Relating to government or public affairs
example: There is a heated political debate about the new tax.
mnemonic: POLITICAL — POLITICS + AL; something related to politics.

meaning_en: Good; hopeful and confident; certain
example: Try to stay positive even when things go wrong.
mnemonic: POSITIVE — POSIT + IVE; something posited with confidence = positive.

meaning_en: The state of being very poor
example: Many charities work to reduce poverty in rural areas.
mnemonic: POVERTY — POOR + VERTY; in poverty, your purse (POVERTY) is empty.

meaning_en: A force pushing on something; a feeling of stress
example: She works well under pressure.
mnemonic: PRESSURE — PRESS + URE; when pressed too hard, you feel pressure.

meaning_en: Coming before in time or order
example: The previous owner of this house renovated the kitchen.
mnemonic: PREVIOUS — PRE + VIOUS; PRE (before) = previous.

meaning_en: A series of steps to achieve a result
example: Learning a language is a long process.
mnemonic: PROCESS — PRO + CESS; a PROfessional goes through a PROCESS.

meaning_en: Something that is made or grown to be sold
example: They launched a new beauty product last month.
mnemonic: PRODUCT — PRO + DUCT; ducts (channels) bring the product to you.

meaning_en: Money earned after costs are paid
example: The company made a large profit last year.
mnemonic: PROFIT — PRO + FIT; a pro stays FIT by making profit.

meaning_en: To encourage something to grow; to move someone to a higher role
example: The campaign promoted healthy eating habits.
mnemonic: PROMOTE — PRO + MOTE; moving forward (pro) = promote.

meaning_en: A public demonstration against something; to object
example: Students marched in protest against the new fee increase.
mnemonic: PROTEST — PRO + TEST; testing (questioning) the PROs of something = protest.

meaning_en: Feeling great satisfaction in something you have done
example: Her parents were proud of her when she topped the class.
mnemonic: PROUD — a LOUD cheer for being PROUD.

meaning_en: To make someone suffer for something wrong they have done
example: The teacher punished the students who were late.
mnemonic: PUNISH — PUN + ISH; a bad PUN is punishable!
